# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash

# Define temporary file path
TEMP_FILE="/tmp/myapp-$$.tmp"

# Write data to temp file
echo "Processing data: data1" > "$TEMP_FILE"
echo "Processing data: data2" >> "$TEMP_FILE"

# Process the data (example: count lines)
LINE_COUNT=$(wc -l < "$TEMP_FILE")

# Read results
echo "Number of lines in the temp file: $LINE_COUNT"

# Process more data, overwriting temp file
echo "Processing data: data3" > "$TEMP_FILE"
echo "Processing data: data4" >> "$TEMP_FILE"

# Read results again
NEW_LINE_COUNT=$(wc -l < "$TEMP_FILE")
echo "New number of lines in the temp file: $NEW_LINE_COUNT"

# Cleanup
rm "$TEMP_FILE"
